name: Swim Day 6: Fast & Short
description: Jess: Short intervals with lots of rest — the closest to 'speed work' that's safe. ~30 min, 1200-1500y. 25s and 50s with plenty of recovery. Gets the competitive swimmer brain happy.

exercises.notes: Drill to activate the catch before the main set.
FAST 25s — not all-out, but 80% effort. Like race pace from back in the day. :20 rest between each. This is the fun part.
Moderate-hard 50s — 70% effort. Focus on turnover and clean strokes. :30 rest between.
Moderate back — keep the tempo up but controlled. :20 rest.
Easy breast kick to flush the legs. Long glides.
Easy sidestroke to bring it home.

tips: 80% effort on the fast 25s — this isn't a race, it's remembering what fast feels like
Plenty of rest between intervals — take more if you need it
Heart rate should come back down during rest before the next one
If you feel breathless or dizzy, dial it back to 60% or skip to the kick flush
The breast kick cooldown after speed work feels incredible
NO flip turns, NO diving off the blocks (tempting, I know), NO butterfly
